en: A: May I ask if Lisin is home?
She’s at home, please come in.

ab: Iraay ci Lisin i loma’ haw?
en: A: May I ask if Lisin is home?

ab: Away ciira i loma’, mimaan kiso?
ch: B:她不在，請問你有什麼事?
en: B: She’s not here, what can I do for you?
B: Who is it?
A: Grandpa, I’m Panay.
B: Who is it?

ab: Kacaw, cimayaw kako, tayniay kako a misalama.
en: A: Kacaw, I’m Mayaw, I’ve come to play with you.

ab: Halafinto a caka saso araw kita.
en: A: Long time no see.

ab: Hayi, ma’ilolay kako ’ tisowanan, katayni misacaciyaw kita.
ch: B:是啊!我很想念你。快來聊天吧!
en: B:Yeah! I miss you so much. Come on, let’s chat!
B: Please sit down and have some tea.
A: Thank you.
B: Please have some tea.

ab: Wa: nga’ay nanomen kona ociya namo.
en: A: Your tea is delicious.
B: Let’s eat pomegranate!

ab: Wa: nengenhan nga’ayay kaenen kona kotamono.
ch: B:好喔!這些水果看起來很棒。
en: A: Yes! These fruits look great.
B: How is the fruit?
A:Yes, it’s really delicious.

ab: Maharekay to ciwina Mitangtang to saka lafi, katayni malafi.
ch: A:媽媽煮好晚餐了，快來吃吧!
en: B: Mom has cooked dinner, come and eat!
A: Okay, I’ll go wash my hands.

ch: A:盡量吃喝，你又不是外人。
en: B: Try to eat and drink as much as possible, you’re not an outsider.

ab: Adihayayto ko mikaenan ako, mafecolay kako.
en: A: I ate a lot, I’m full.
B: Eat more, don’t be polite.
A:Well, it’s delicious.

ab: Miliyasto kako, liyaw sato kako a tayni miliso’ tisowanan.
ch: A:我要離開了，下次再來看你。
en: A: I’m leaving, I’ll see you next time.
B: Okay, goodbye.
A: It’s late, I’m going home.
B:Come again next time!
B: Walk slowly and carefully.

ab: Hayda, ano ira koromi’ad tayni sato misalama.
en: A: Okay, come to my house sometime.
